behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies: correctness is the model’s produced signature, not just wrapper logic. A stubbed model can always emit the expected signature and hide real failures in interpretation of natural-language specs. Rejected higher level Workflow because the behavior is a single generation-quality check, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run real LLM generation for a prompt with strict interface requirements, parse the produced function signature, and assert parameter names/types/return type match the expected interface.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: Integration-check: cross-module handoff phrase is "Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt." Unit is disqualified because this requires wiring between attempt generation/execution/result handling, not only one local function. Higher levels are disqualified: no real OS/network/subprocess is required, and no dependence on real AI output quality; deterministic pass/fail stubs are sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with stub generator and stub test runner that fail then pass; assert call order is attempt 1 then 2, attempt 3 is never executed, and the returned result is from the first passing attempt.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires a live iterative loop where multiple agentic steps are sequenced and later steps depend on earlier failures. LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP both apply. Rejected Agentic because one-step model evaluation cannot verify the full generate→fail→refine→pass sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real LLM and real iterative controller on a known prompt where first attempt often fails; run full loop up to max attempts and assert at least one later attempt passes within the allowed limit.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Integration-check: cross-module handoff phrase is "When tests fail" + "includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt." Unit is disqualified because real failure can occur at the boundary where test-result objects are transformed and passed to prompt-building logic. Higher levels are disqualified: no real OS/network/subprocess is needed, and this behavior checks prompt content assembly, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run failure results through the real test-result-to-refinement-prompt path and assert the produced prompt contains each failed test name and failure message verbatim/structured as expected.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires a live second attempt conditioned on previous failure context, so it is an agentic handoff across sequential steps. A mocked model can trivially return different code and hide whether real model behavior responds to failure feedback. Rejected Agentic because single-call evaluation cannot validate context-carryover across attempts.
behaviors.test_description: Execute two real LLM attempts where the first fails tests; feed actual failure context into refinement and assert second generated implementation materially differs and addresses prior failure signal (not identical resubmission).

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: Integration-check: cross-module handoff phrase is "returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results." Unit is disqualified because the failure appears when loop control, attempt bookkeeping, and result aggregation interact. Higher levels are disqualified: no requirement for real OS/network/subprocess or real model judgment; deterministic failures from stubs can exercise the boundary exactly.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic failing attempt outputs for three iterations and assert the orchestrator halts at 3, emits the expected structured error schema, and includes each attempt’s recorded test results.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Integration-check: cross-module handoff phrase is "full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end." Unit is disqualified because single-function tests cannot validate end-to-end module wiring. Higher levels are disqualified by explicit text "non-agentic" and "deterministic stubbed model outputs," which removes model-quality dependence; real OS/network/subprocess is not required by the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire generator adapter, test executor interface, and attempt tracker with deterministic stub model outputs and deterministic test outcomes; run end-to-end and assert expected transitions and final result.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a full live multi-step loop with sequential model-dependent decisions (generate→test→refine→retest). MULTI-STEP and LLM-DEP both apply, so lower levels miss real failures in iterative agent behavior. Rejected Agentic because one-step model checks cannot validate end-to-end iterative recovery on a hard task.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real loop on a curated hard function benchmark known to fail first attempt; require first attempt fail, later attempt pass, and final produced code satisfies the test suite.
